sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Danaher Corporation $DHR Shares Purchased by Mutual of America Capital Management LLC

feed.title: Keysight supports Airbus UpNext SpaceRAN 5G NTN tests
feed.summary: Keysight Technologies is collaborating with Airbus UpNext on the SpaceRAN demonstrator, an initiative to explore 5G non-terrestrial networks (NTN) using software-defined satellite technology. Keysight is providing crucial test and measurement solutions, including satellite channel and user segment emulation, to validate key 5G NTN use cases like beam and gateway handover and mobility management between terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks. This project aims to integrate 5G NTN capabilities for future global broadband and direct-to-handheld services from space.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (STLD) Steel Dynamics, Inc. Reports Q4 Revenue $4.41B, vs. FactSet Est of $4.55B

The report also highlights the company's Q4 earnings, net sales increase, and an EPS of $1.82, which was below FactSet's estimate of $1.91. The article provides additional related news and company financial details.

feed.title: Home Depot’s key to great AI experiences? Ensure it remains an authority
feed.summary: Home Depot is proactively integrating AI into its customer experience to maintain its brand authority and build trust, rather than adopting a "wait-and-see" approach. The company focuses on ensuring AI interactions reflect its brand voice and high customer service standards, treating AI agents like in-store associates. Home Depot also prioritizes transparency about AI usage, experimenting with clear but subtle prompts to manage customer expectations and foster trust.

feed.title: Domino's preps for football Sunday with 2.4M pizzas and $6.99 deal
feed.summary: Domino's launched a limited-time carryout special offering large two-topping pizzas for $6.99 from January 26 to February 1, 2026, anticipating the biggest football Sunday, which is typically one of its top five busiest days. The company expects to sell around 2.4 million pizzas on game day, about 40% more than a normal Sunday. This promotion continues Domino's strategy of offering value deals around key consumption periods.

feed.title: Domino's® Throws Game-Winning Pass with Carryout Special
feed.summary: Domino's is offering a special carryout deal of large two-topping pizzas for $6.99 each from January 26 to February 1, ahead of the biggest football Sunday of the year. This promotion helps the company prepare for one of its busiest days, which typically sees sales of around 2.4 million pizzas, 40% more than a normal Sunday. Customers can choose various crusts, with options for specialty and stuffed crust pizzas at an additional charge.

feed.title: Brown & Brown mourns death of chief legal officer Robert Mathis
feed.summary: Brown & Brown has announced the death of Robert Mathis, the company's chief legal officer, who passed away after nearly three years in the role. CEO Powell Brown praised Mathis's judgment, integrity, and kindness, noting his significant contributions to the company. Eileen Akerson will serve as interim chief legal officer, and Mathis's extensive legal career spanned over three decades across various companies and geographies.

feed.title: Gartner: Only One-Third of Consumers Say GenAI Rivals Search Engines
feed.summary: Gartner's research indicates that only about one-third of consumers find GenAI chatbots as effective as traditional search engines for learning new information. Despite this, AI features are extending the research journey for many, with 31% spending more time searching due to AI summaries. The report emphasizes that marketers must continue to optimize content for both AI-driven answers and classic search results, adapting to evolving consumer behaviors and the rise of agentic AI for personalized interactions.

feed.title: IBM Earnings Preview: Operating Margin Is Slowly Expanding
feed.summary: IBM is expected to report its Q4 2025 financial results on January 28th, 2026, with consensus estimates predicting significant year-over-year growth in EPS, revenue, and operating income. The company's operating margin has shown slow expansion, and shrinking capital expenditures have boosted free cash flow. Key drivers for IBM's performance include expanding operating margins, a focus on software and AI, and successful integration of RedHat, while its capital allocation strategy prioritizes debt reduction over share repurchases.

feed.title: Moderna to Report Fourth Quarter 2025 Financial Results on Friday, February 13, 2026
feed.summary: Moderna, Inc. announced it will host a live conference call and webcast on Friday, February 13, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. ET to release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results and provide a corporate update. The webcast will be available on the Investors section of Moderna's website, with an archived version accessible for one year. The company focuses on pioneering mRNA medicine to develop vaccines and therapeutics for various diseases.

feed.title: What Analysts Are Saying About Solventum Stock
feed.summary: Six analysts have evaluated Solventum (NYSE: SOLV) over the past three months, resulting in an average price target of $96.50, a 12.21% increase from the previous average. Solventum, spun out from 3M in early 2024, shows strong financial metrics including a high net margin, ROE, and ROA, alongside a prudent debt management strategy.
